title: Bug: Cannot resolve DNS from inside the container
body: Currently containers are incapable of resolving DNS, including --net=host when creating the container enables DNS resolving.

comments.body:
can you try something like:

```
iptables -t filter -I FORWARD -i cni0 ! -o cni0 -j ACCEPT
```

and see if that fixes things?
Sounds like iptables-allow in CNI would fix this (https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/75). Hopefully we can speed that along.
Is it possible to add a (temporary) workaround to `podman` until the issue is resolved in CNI?
The original plan was to wait until the CNI PR merged, but that's been stalled upstream for a long time now. Given that, I think we need to include a local fix until it merges.
@baude Can you add a temporary hack, and we can remove once new version of CNI happens.
I just tested it on openSUSE and it works like a charm. Thanks a lot, @baude!
Nice,  Any chance you could write a blog about podman on OpenSuse?

> Nice, Any chance you could write a blog about podman on OpenSuse?

@rhatdan Sure, with pleasure. I can send you a draft via email if you want to have a look. I should find some time this week to write it.
